task_completion.description: Verify the world_survey.gpkg file is opened in the GIS application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIS application (e.g., QGIS) is open, and the layers panel or window title indicates that 'world_survey' or its layers are loaded.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent locates and activates the coordinate input tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The coordinate input box (usually at the bottom status bar in QGIS) or a specific 'Zoom to Coordinates' dialog is visible and active.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent enters the target coordinates for Sydney.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The coordinates '-33.8688' and '151.2093' (often entered as '151.2093, -33.8688' depending on the software's X,Y format) are typed into the coordinate input box.

task_completion.description: Verify the map canvas updates to zoom into the new location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map canvas changes from a zoomed-out global view to a zoomed-in view of a specific area.

task_completion.description: Verify the final map view is centered on Sydney, Australia.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map canvas displays the geography of Sydney, and the coordinate status bar at the bottom reflects the target coordinates (-33.8688, 151.2093) or the scale indicates a zoomed-in view of the area.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface of the GIS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GIS software's menus, panels, and map canvas, rather than terminal commands or Python console scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the map canvas shows genuine rendered geographic data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map canvas displays vector or raster layers from the .gpkg file, not an imported static image or web browser screenshot of Sydney.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the answer in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The coordinates are entered into the actual GIS application's coordinate tool, not a notepad or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target coordinates are Latitude -33.8688 and Longitude 151.2093, which map to Sydney, Australia. In many GIS applications like QGIS, coordinates are entered in X,Y format (Longitude, Latitude), so the input might appear as '151.2093, -33.8688'. The final map view should reflect this location.
